item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was on the fence, but I'm grasping that the "empty" time between iterations is the time before someone views the tape again. That long gap referred to was caused by the Foundation's mercy policy of suspending testing. That made it complete for me. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the parallels between these poor unfortuantes and Lord Bright of the "Doctors of the Church" continuity.
They all just want off the reincarnation merry-go-round so they can go back to relatively sweet oblivion, brutal suicide is just the quickest option.

While this is an interesting and well-executed snapshot of their experience, it would be interesting to see some of the other reaction periods. That couple dozen iterations when they form a free-love cult; or the subjective years when everyone just sits silently in place and watches the clock; or how they have so memorized every detail of each other that they are de facto psychic within the group and invent a new science. In an infinite (albeit sharply constricted) life, humans will eventually try everything at least once.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While I agree it is redundant, it is well executed and good enough to stand on its own. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To add on to what Roget and Smapti had to say, I don't feel this brings anything to the table that the SCP article itself doesn't. It's quite well-written, but it feels redundant with the log as originally presented.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh. I think the actual article does a much better job of this story. Personally, I think it'd be more interesting if they come to terms with their situation and just played the game. Not really scary, or interesting, just competently executed. It might work for some people, but not me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked the original, but this is just too cliche and maudlin. I've seen "it keeps happening over and over again, WHY WON'T YOU LET ME DIE" far too many times to do anything but groan when I see it.

Frankly, it'd be much more interesting to me if the attendees eventually came to grips with the nature of their existence and established a society within the confines of the loop.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted. Loved the SCP and I love this. Minor thing:

But just image: We could have just been out forever, and all the things that happened after they started trying to kill the kids would have never happened.

"Image" should probably be "imagine" and you don't need to capitalize "We" in that sentence.

Very, very, awesome.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm just gonna curl up for a bit

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's been a while since I gave myself chills while reading over something I wrote.

Enjoy.
